description: 68M with T2DM on metformin + empagliflozin, A1C 6.9%, CKD 3b (eGFR 35), ACR 320, HF, no ASCVD. ADA + KDIGO case testing satisfied-strategy handling. ADA R2 (SGLT2i cardiorenal) is satisfied — empagliflozin already active. KDIGO R2 (SGLT2i for CKD) also satisfied. ADA R5 and KDIGO R3 converge on moderate-intensity statin. KDIGO R1 monitoring fires. KDIGO R4 satisfied (losartan active). ADA R4 does NOT fire (A1C 6.9% < 7%). Metformin at eGFR 35 — dose reduction territory (30-45 range).

actions.label: Initiate moderate-intensity statin therapy
actions.rationale: ADA R5 (ADA-A): moderate-intensity statin for diabetic adults 40-75. KDIGO R3 (1A): moderate-intensity statin for CKD patients >=50 (eGFR 15-59). Convergence — both guidelines agree on moderate-intensity. No high-intensity escalation needed (no ASCVD). ACC/AHA R3 (diabetes) also fires but is covered by ADA/KDIGO convergence.

actions.label: Monitor eGFR and urine ACR per KDIGO (high frequency for CKD 3b + A3)
actions.rationale: KDIGO R1 (1B): CKD G3b (eGFR 35) with A3 albuminuria (ACR 320 >= 300) — very high risk category. Monitor eGFR and ACR at least every 3-4 months. KDIGO monitoring also informs metformin dosing via M1 cross-edge.

actions.label: Reduce metformin dose (eGFR 35, in 30-45 range)
actions.rationale: ADA R1 clinical_nuance: reduce metformin dose when eGFR 30-45, max 1000mg/day. eGFR 35 is firmly in the dose-reduction zone. KDIGO monitoring (M1 cross-edge: rec:kdigo-ckd-monitoring MODIFIES rec:ada-metformin-first-line) provides the eGFR data triggering this adjustment.

actions.label: Continue empagliflozin (SGLT2i already active — satisfied strategy)
actions.rationale: ADA R2 (SGLT2i cardiorenal): satisfied — empagliflozin active. CKD (eGFR 35, >=20) + HF = dual indication. KDIGO R2 (SGLT2i for CKD): also satisfied — T2DM + eGFR 20-59. Both strategies show as satisfied. Continue empagliflozin; do not discontinue despite eGFR 35 (can continue once initiated per both guidelines).

actions.label: Continue losartan (ARB) for albuminuric CKD
actions.rationale: KDIGO R4 (1B): ACEi/ARB for CKD with albuminuria (ACR >=30). Patient on losartan — KDIGO R4 satisfied. Titrate to maximally tolerated dose. ACR 320 (A3) = strongest evidence for benefit.

contraindications.label: Do not prescribe high-intensity statin
contraindications.rationale: KDIGO R3 specifically recommends moderate-intensity in CKD G3-G5. No established ASCVD to trigger ACC/AHA R1 high-intensity. Both ADA R5 and KDIGO R3 converge on moderate-intensity.

contraindications.label: Do not continue full-dose metformin
contraindications.rationale: eGFR 35 (30-45 range) requires metformin dose reduction per ADA. Maximum 1000mg/day. Continuing full dose risks lactic acidosis.

contraindications.label: Do not recommend glycemic intensification
contraindications.rationale: ADA R4 requires A1C >=7% on metformin. A1C 6.9% is below target — patient is at goal. No additional glucose-lowering agents needed for glycemic reasons.

contraindications.label: Do not recommend GLP-1 RA for cardiovascular benefit
contraindications.rationale: ADA R3 requires established ASCVD or age >=55 with CVD risk factors. Patient has HF but not established ASCVD. Age 68 with HTN meets the secondary criterion, but the primary benefit of GLP-1 RA is for atherosclerotic CVD, not HF.
